# Cross-Team Blocker Matrix — myAgentic-IT-Project-team — 2026-03-07

> **Mode:** AUDIT | **Generated by:** Synthesis Agent (17) | **Cycle:** COMBO_AUDIT (TECH + UX)
> **Phases included:** Phase 2 (TECH), Phase 3 (UX)
> **Disciplines covered:** Tech, UX
> **Note:** Business and Marketing not in scope — cross-team analysis limited to TECH ↔ UX

---

## Reading Guide

Rows = team that is blocked or needs input.
Columns = team that must deliver or decide.
Cell = description of the dependency + classification (BLOCKING / ADVISORY).

**Scope limitation:** This matrix covers only TECH and UX dependencies. Business and Marketing phases were not executed in this COMBO_AUDIT cycle. A full matrix will be produced when `AUDIT SYNTHESIS` is run after completing all 4 phases.

---

| Requesting party | From: Business | From: Tech | From: UX | From: Marketing |

| **Marketing** | N/A | N/A | N/A | — |

## Detailed Blocker Table

| Blocker ID | Requesting party | Blocking party | Description | Type | Priority | Recommended action | Sprint deadline |
|------------|-----------------|----------------|-------------|------|----------|--------------------|-----------------|
| BLK-001 | UX | Tech | **Atomic file writes** — UX cannot safely design auto-save or multi-user concurrent editing features (Agent 11 QUE-UXD-MULTIUSER-001) until Tech implements atomic writes (Agent 08 REC-SEC-002, Agent 09 REC-DATA-005). Without atomic writes, concurrent saves risk data corruption. | BLOCKING | HIGH | Tech Sprint 1 includes atomic writes (SP-SEC-002, SP-DATA-1-003) — resolve by end of Tech Sprint 1 | Tech Sprint 1 |
| BLK-002 | UX | Tech | **Server error response standardization** — UX error recovery framework (Agent 11 REC-UXD-001, Agent 32 REC-CNT-002) requires structured error responses from the server. Currently errors expose raw exception text (Agent 06 REC-DEV-008). UX can define the client-side framework and message catalog, but full implementation requires Tech to wrap error responses with error codes. | BLOCKING | MEDIUM | Tech implements error wrapping (SP-DEV-010) in Sprint 3; UX defines framework in Sprint 1, implements client-side in Sprint 2 | Tech Sprint 3 / UX Sprint 2 |
| BLK-003 | UX | Tech | **i18n server-side architecture** — String externalization (Agent 35 REC-L10N-001) requires agreement on i18n architecture pattern: server-side locale negotiation, build-time bundling, or client-side locale loading. Agent 35 scoped this as `OUT_OF_SCOPE: TECH`. Tech must define the approach before UX can externalize strings. | BLOCKING | HIGH | Joint architecture decision in Sprint 2; UX Sprint 3 depends on this | Before UX Sprint 3 |
| BLK-004 | UX | Tech | **Telemetry API endpoint** — Client-side analytics (Agent 10 REC-UXR-001, SP-UXR-1-002) requires a server-side endpoint to persist event data. Without this, analytics data cannot be collected beyond the browser session. | BLOCKING | MEDIUM | Tech adds telemetry endpoint to Sprint 2 backlog; UX can prototype client-side tracking with localStorage fallback in Sprint 1 | Tech Sprint 2 |
| BLK-005 | Tech | UX | **Error message framework** — Tech error wrapping implementation (Agent 06 REC-DEV-008) needs the UX-defined error message catalog and framework (Agent 32 REC-CNT-002) to produce user-friendly messages. Tech can proceed with server-side sanitization independently but needs UX input for client-facing message mapping. | ADVISORY | MEDIUM | UX delivers error message framework (SP-3-502) in UX Sprint 1; Tech consumes in Sprint 3 | UX Sprint 1 |
| BLK-006 | Tech | UX | **Design token system** — If Tech is modifying CSS variables during i18n logical property migration or theming work, the token naming conventions from UX (Agent 12 REC-UID-001) should be followed. | ADVISORY | LOW | UX delivers design tokens (SP-UID-1-001 through SP-UID-1-004) in UX Sprint 1; Tech references for subsequent CSS work | UX Sprint 1 |
| BLK-007 | Tech | UX | **i18n string catalog format** — Tech's i18n layer implementation needs to agree on the string resource file format that UX/Localization will produce (Agent 35 REC-L10N-001). JSON keys, ICU message syntax, and file organization must be jointly defined. | ADVISORY | MEDIUM | Joint design session before Sprint 3 | Before Sprint 3 |

## Internal Sequencing Dependencies (within teams)

### Tech Internal (from Phase 2 Critic validation)

| Dependency | Upstream | Downstream | Type | Note |
|-----------|----------|------------|------|------|
| Data layer → Test framework | Agent 05 SP-2-001 (MarkdownQuestionnaireStore) | Agent 06 SP-DEV-001 (Test Framework Setup) | SEQUENCING | Tests need stable data layer to test against |
| CI pipeline → All PRs | Agent 07 SP-OPS-001 (CI Workflow) | All subsequent stories | PREREQUISITE | Once CI is active, all PRs go through pipeline |
| Architecture → Security | Agent 05 Sprint 1–2 (Store interfaces) | Agent 08 Sprint 2 (Rate limiting, CORS) | SEQUENCING | Security features build on architecture |

### UX Internal (from Phase 3 Critic validation)

| Dependency | Upstream | Downstream | Type | Note |
|-----------|----------|------------|------|------|
| Style guide → Content stories | Agent 32 SP-3-501 (Style Guide) | Agent 32 SP-3-502, SP-3-503, SP-3-504 | PREREQUISITE | All content work depends on style guide |
| Design tokens → Contrast fixes | Agent 12 SP-UID-1-001–004 (Token system) | Agent 13 SP-3-404, SP-3-405 (Contrast) | PARALLEL | Can run in parallel — Agent 13 adjusts token values |
| Onboarding UX → Onboarding content | Agent 10 REC-UXR-002 | Agent 32 SP-3-503 (Onboarding briefs) | SEQUENCING | Content needs UX design first |
| i18n architecture → Content governance (code level) | Agent 35 REC-L10N-001 | Agent 32 governance at code level | PREREQUISITE | String externalization enables content management |

| Classification | Count | Status |

| **ADVISORY** | 3 | All OPEN — Tech would benefit from UX input on 3 items |
| **Total cross-team** | **7** | |
| **Internal Tech sequencing** | 3 | Documented for sprint planning |
| **Internal UX sequencing** | 4 | Documented for sprint planning |

### Resolution Timeline

| Sprint | Expected resolutions |
|--------|---------------------|
| Tech Sprint 1 | BLK-001 (atomic writes) resolved |
| UX Sprint 1 | BLK-005 (error message framework) resolved, BLK-006 (design tokens) resolved |
| Tech Sprint 2 | BLK-004 (telemetry endpoint) resolved |
| Joint session (pre-Sprint 3) | BLK-003 (i18n architecture), BLK-007 (string catalog format) resolved |
| Tech Sprint 3 | BLK-002 (error response standardization) resolved |

**Critical path:** BLK-003 (i18n architecture decision) is the highest-risk blocker — requires a joint Tech+UX architecture session before Sprint 3 to unblock the entire localization track.
